notes: subaru.com/vehicles/ consumer site. Solterra is the EV; Trailseeker and Uncharted are 2026 additions (Trailseeker is the Outback successor for off-road trim, Uncharted is a new compact EV). Subaru's CDN at s7d1.scene7.com/is/image/scomv2/ uses 3-letter model codes (OBK/CTK/FOR/BRZ/WRX/SOL/TSK/UNC/ASC/IMP) in the URL path, NOT the consumer model slug. Without slug_variants the slug filter rejects 100% of candidates for 8 of 10 models (only BRZ and WRX self-match). Phase A3 investigation (session 7) added slug_variants for trailseeker only — see reports/subaru_angle_patterns_session7.md for why the others were intentionally left at their structural baseline. Subaru also serves extension-less Scene7 URLs (e.g. `...?$2000w$` with no `.jpg`); the production scraper's `` extension check rejects these, so the alt-text-carrying tag inside is filtered out and only the entries (which bypass the check) survive — meaning slug-matching cannot benefit from text on this brand. The MY26__jelly_3247x1224 file (BRZ/WRX/OBK/FOR/CTK/SOL/ASC/IMP/UNC) is a studio cut-out SIDE PROFILE, not a front-3/4 — visually confirmed by downloading and inspecting all 8. The 9 existing positional-fallback successes on BRZ/WRX (currently labeled front_three_quarter) are actually side_profile cuts saved under the wrong angle. Fixing this requires a scrape_image_urls.mjs change to teach pickByPosition about brand patterns; out of scope for Phase A3.
